Keywords: brand awareness; communication technology; customer engagement; metaverse; metaverse user experience; virtual world

Authors: Ratih Amelia; Achmad Kautsar; Dewie Tri Wijayati Wardoyo
Title: Digital Marketing Strategy for Customer Loyalty with the Role of Brand Positioning and Trust Mediation
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: This Strategically setting digital marketing objectives enables the marketing team and firm to imagine the best techniques and approaches to achieving them. They provide everyone involved with a sense of purpose and direction, ensuring that everyone works towards the same goal. Companies must determine a digital marketing strategy to increase repurchase intention. The purpose of this study is to find out the steps that companies must take in increasing repurchase intention. In previous research, brand positioning variables can affect repurchase intention. Companies that have implemented digital marketing strategies require consumer trust in increasing repurchase intention. The research will be carried out in Small and Medium Enterprises in the scope of Trowulan Mojokerto sculpture craft. Trowulan is the largest sculpture producing city in East Java. This study employed purposive sampling. All prawn farming firms that meet the criteria for this study. This study's data collection techniques included a questionnaire and a literature review. The research instrument utilised in this study was a questionnaire administered to respondents using five Likert scales. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: brand positioning; customer loyalty; digital marketing strategy; role of branding positioning

Authors: Fresha Kharisma; Ratih Amalia; Ika Diyah Candra Arifah; Nadia Nur Thahirrah
Title: Live Streaming on Marketplace: User Experience, Credibility, Parasocial Interaction Toward Purchase Intention
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: The rise in internet users has prompted individuals to become live streamers and promote products through online market places. Live streaming creates a unique attraction and holds the potential to influence consumer purchasing decisions. This study aims to identify the key factors that marketers should focus on when promoting their products in order to enhance consumer purchase intentions. This research employs a quantitative survey method by distributing questionnaires to marketplace consumers who have both watched live streams and made purchases on the platform. Testing using a Statistical Package for the Social Sciences (SPSS) shows the results The factors influencing consumer purchase intentions are crucial aspects that marketers must carefully consider. which shows the influence between user experience, credibility, parasocial interaction on purchase intention. Therefore, user experience, credibility, and parasocial interaction must be considered by digital marketers in the marketplace to increase consumer purchase intention. This study is expected to provide managerial influence in the form of contributions to the marketplace regarding how effective live streaming digital marketing can reach consumers and inform them about new products, offers, discounts, or promotions that increase conversion rates in the marketplace. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: credibility; live streaming; market place; parasocial interaction; purchase intention; user experience

Authors: Dhiyan Septa Wihara; Hafid Kholidi Hadi; Ratih Amelia; Muhammad Fachmi
Title: ICT Implementations: Typology of Using Digital Platforms for Efficient Business Performance in Microfinance Institutions a Bibliometrix Perspective
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: The most complete application of ICT is currently only in microfinance on a large scale, including internet banking and mobile banking, cloud computing, big data analysis and blockchain. Meanwhile, in microfinance institutions, the digital platforms used are limited to mobile phones with internet-based online platforms to simplify operational performance. This research aims first to map research on ICT implementation around the world published in reputable international journals and indexed by Scopus. Second, analyzing the network of variable relationships in ICT implementation. Third, to analyze digital platform use in microfinance institutions (MFIs). The results of this study in the last 21 years from 2003-2024 only found 83 articles on ICT implementation in microfinance. Then there are 4 research clusters related to ICT implementation in microfinance. Furthermore, the digital platforms most widely used by microfinance are internet banking and mobile banking at 79.92%. While big data analysis, cloud computing and block chain are still far below 20%. This research is an initial step in mapping several variables and indicators for further development, especially ICT implementation in Indonesia, which is still very rarely done. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: blockchain; cloud computing; information and communication technolog (ict); internet banking; microfinance institution (mfis); mobile banking

Authors: Achmad Kautsar; Nadia Nur Thahirrah; Anita Safitri; Muhammad Fajar Wahyudi Rahman; Dewie Tri Wijayati; Ratih Amelia
Title: Computational Mathematics of Risk and Return Portofolio Banking (Ramadhan Effect 2024)
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: Financial engineering combines a rigorous study of computational mathematics with economics and quantitative finance. This research was conducted to analyze the level of risk and return of banking stock portfolios after the Ramadan effect. This research uses a descriptive event study method by calculating the 3 most profitable stocks in the banking sector to be used as a portfolio with a weight of 33% each. The results showed that the expected return for the banking portfolio was 0.27% and the predicted risk in the next period was 1.46%. These results have been calculated carefully to provide an illustration that investors who want to buy a portfolio of banking shares do not need to worry about future returns. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: banking; financial engineering; portofolio; return; risk

Authors: Ratih Amelia; Fresha Kharisma; Nadia Nur Thahirrah; Dhiyan Septa Wihara; Raya Sulistyowati; Ika Diyah Candra
Title: Social Media Marketing and Electronic Word of Mouth on Customer Preference and Choice in Over-the-Top Media Service Platform
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: The increase in the number of top media service platforms in Indonesia is an interesting phenomenon to be studied. The large selection of top media service platforms requires companies to compete for consumers. The choice of the right marketing tools in attracting consumers to choose the top media service platform is social media marketing and electronic word of mouth. In digital marketing strategy, electronic word of mouth is one of the considerations of consumers in choosing the products they will consume. This study employed a quantitative research methodology. Basic research, or pure research, is included in this study. The study will be conducted among Netflix subscribers. A questionnaire and a review of the literature were used as data gathering methods in this investigation. Respondents to the study were given a questionnaire with five Likert scales as the research tool. The research concludes that Social Media Marketing does not significantly influence Customer Preference or Customer Choice, while E-WOM (Electronic Word of Mouth) has a substantial impact on both. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: customer preference; electronic word of mouth; social media marketing; top media service platform

Authors: Ratih Amelia; Ahmad Kurniawan; Hujjatullah Fazlurrahman; Ainul Huda Binti Jamil; Achmad Kautsar
Title: Digital Banking and Sustainable Financial Environment to Improve Financial Performance in SMEs: Systematic Literature Review
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: This systematic literature review aims to examine the role of digital banking and the development of a sustainable financial environment in enhancing the financial performance of Small and Medium-sized Enterprises (SMEs). The review synthesizes existing research to highlight key findings, emerging themes, and gaps, providing a comprehensive understanding of how digital banking and sustainable financial practices impact SME financial performance. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: digital banking; improve financial performance; small and medium-sized enterprises (SMes); sustainable financial environment

Authors: Achmad Kautsar; Ina Uswatun Nihaya; Muhammad Fajar Wahyudi Rahman; Hafid Kholidi Hadi; Anita Safitri; Dewie Tri Wijayati
Title: Distance Learning for Community Service Program (KKN) Students: Finance Courses
Journal: 2024 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems
Abstract: Community Service Program (KKN) Merdeka Belajar aims to develop their student independence in planning, implementing and evaluating their projects. In implementing this distance learning program, Surabaya State University in the management study program has a policy of continuing to ask students to achieve the outcomes of the courses taken in that semester. This research used an online survey method with 250 management study program student respondents who were taking financial report analysis course and budgeting course, where they were spread across KKN village locations in the East Java province. The results indicate that the majority of students still need comprehensive assistance from lecturers so that the program can be achieved in the community according to the courses they are taking that semester. Future implications, distance learning has experienced a transformative phase due to advances in digital technology. Technological advances are taking an important role in learning with the emergence of innovative learning platforms and tools.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: community service program (KKN) Merdeka Belajar; distance learning; e-learning; finance

Authors: Achmad Kautsar; Muhammad Madyan; Rahmat Setiawan
Title: Literature Review of Digital Transformation on Banking: Corporate Perspective
Journal: 2024 International Conference on Sustainable Islamic Business and Finance
Abstract: This research focus on discussing the extent of the impact of digital transformation on the performance of the banking industry in corporate area. In addition, this research will contribute to describing the development of digital transformation occurring in the banking industry and future research directions. This research uses a bibliometric approach to identify and map research trends in the latest era using PRISMA diagrams and VOS viewer. This research contributes to a clear measuring tool for digital transformation using the Index of Digital Transformation. Our findings also show that there is a research gap that exists between several studies that form the basis, so that in the future these variables can be tested for the development of new research models. Future research directions show that Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG), credit risk, agency costs, and industrial banking factors can be developed as main considerations.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: Credit Risk; Digital Transformation; ESG; Industrial Banking

Authors: Fresha Kharisma; Ika Diyah Candra Arifah; Achmad Kautsar; Renny Sari Dewi
Title: Digital Marketing, Brand Awareness and Customer Relationship Management on Repurchase Intention in E-Commerce Marketplace
Journal: ICBIR 2024 - 2024 9th International Conference on Business and Industrial Research
Abstract: The development of technology and information has changed consumer behavior. Hence, the marketing strategy challenge lies in how effectively it can increase awareness, conversion, and repurchase behavior as the determinants of e-commerce business sustainability. This study explores the influence of digital marketing, brand awareness, and customer relationship management on repurchase intention, with brand trust mediating variables in the e-commerce marketplace. The research analysis tool is SmartPLS 3.0, which shows an influence between digital marketing, customer relationship marketing, and brand trust on repurchase intention. Hence, digital marketing, customer relationship management, and brand awareness must be considered by digital marketers in the e-commerce marketplace to increase repurchase intention. The research is expected to provide managerial implications in the form of contributions to the e-commerce marketplace regarding how effectively digital marketing can reach consumers and inform them about new products, offers, discounts, or promotions that increase conversion rates in the e-commerce marketplace. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: brand awareness; brand trust; customer relationship management; digital marketing; repurchase intention

Authors: Hafid Kholidi Hadi; Anita Safitri; Dhiyan Septa Wihara; Achmad Kautsar; Dicka Widya Purnama; Dwi Yoga Wicaksana
Title: Unveiling the Impact of AI-Mediated Interactions on Student Outcomes in Online Learning: A Bibliometric Analysis
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: The educational landscape is rapidly evolving, with a growing emphasis on integrating technology into the learning process. However, the effectiveness of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) in enhancing student performance remains a topic of debate. This study delves deeper by specifically examining the influence of interactions between instructors and students mediated by Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems on student performance and anxiety within online learning environments. Utilizing bibliometric analysis techniques, this research offers a systematic analysis of existing literature to identify trends, gaps, and patterns in the research surrounding technology-driven education. By focusing on specific disciplines like mathematics and literacy, the study aims to reveal potential variations in the impact of technology based on subject matter and educational level. This analysis is expected to provide novel insights to guide the design and implementation of educational technology that fosters effective and inclusive learning. Additionally, the research intends to offer practical recommendations for optimal technology integration in various educational contexts (data analysis, business processes, etc.). Ultimately, this study aspires to contribute to improved educational quality through the strategic and impactful utilization of technology. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: artificial intelligence; information and communication technology; online learning; student outcome

Authors: Nadia Asandimitra; Achmad Kautsar; Dewie Tri Wijayati; Nunik Dwi Kusumawati; Ina Uswatun Nihaya
Title: WOMEN IN BUSINESS: THE IMPACT OF DIGITAL AND FINANCIAL LITERACY ON FEMALE-OWNED SMALL AND MEDIUM-SIZED ENTERPRISES
Journal: Investment Management and Financial Innovations
Abstract: The growth of small and medium enterprises (SMEs) shows a positive trend and has even become a major highlight in the national economic landscape. Moreover, most of these businesses are owned by women. Examining how digital capability and financial literacy and a dynamic environment affect the success of female-owned small and medium-sized enterprises in Indonesia is the main goal of this study. Financial and digital capability are examined as mediators between performance and these factors. The study used a causal explanatory research approach. The data in this study were collected through questionnaires. This research surveyed 100 female-owned small and medium-sized enterprises in Indonesia’s food and beverage, herbal and pharmaceutical, fashion, and craft industries. Partial Least Square (PLS) analysis was utilized in this work as an analytical tool. The study reveals that financial literacy significantly enhances SMEs` performance by improving resource management. However, a dynamic environment alone does not impact performance unless supported by other competencies. Financial competence does not strengthen the effect of financial literacy, indicating it may not be sufficient for success. Conversely, digital competence is crucial, mediating the relationship between a dynamic environment and SMEs` performance. Therefore, SMEs with strong digital skills are better positioned to adapt and thrive, making digital competence key to sustained business success. © Nadia Asandimitra, Achmad Kautsar, Dewie Tri Wijayati, Nunik Dwi Kusumawati, Ina Uswatun Nihaya, 2024.
Keywords: digital capability; dynamic environment; female-owned enterprises; financial capability; financial literacy

Authors: Tony Seno Aji; Jun Surjanti; Rahayu Dewi Soeyono; Achmad Kautsar
Title: Enhancement Business Capabilities Through Cooperative Learning: Economic Improvement for Culinary E-Business Owners
Journal: 2024 ASU International Conference in Emerging Technologies for Sustainability and Intelligent Systems
Abstract: Surabaya, as a metropolitan city, shows various e-business developments, one of which is culinary. For these, Surabaya's government creates culinary centers for small business actors (street vendors). Besides, the government also attempts to increase knowledge of those small business actors on sustainable business especially in culinary through training and workshop. This study aims to analyze the improvement of business capabilities through Cooperative Learning workshops with Student Team Achievement Divisions (STAD) on small business actors (street vendors) culinary in Surabaya in order to create Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Participants were 120 street vendors meeting the criteria to join the workshop on sustainable culinary e-business. The result indicates that a workshop conducted using Cooperative Learning STAD capably improves knowledge of street vendors on sustainable business (94% of achievement level). To increase achievement levels, further studies on factors influencing its success should be conducted. Furthermore, it serves as a reference for policymakers and implementers in planning and designing materials and workshops or training on culinary street vendors and other SMEs.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: Business Capabilities; Culinary Street Vendor; e-business; Student Team Achievement Divisions (STAD); Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Authors: Udisubakti Ciptomulyono; Hujjatullah Fazlurrahman; Reza Aulia Akbar; Ninditya Nareswari
Title: Green human resource management in Sustainable University: Systematic literature review
Journal: E3S Web of Conferences
Abstract: The concept of environmentally friendly human resource management (GHRM) within the university corporate sector has emerged as a contemporary and intriguing research topic. The push to develop research in sustainability management is ongoing, focusing on raising awareness about the importance of adopting environmentally friendly practices in university corporate services. This research aims to explore the developments in GHRM research over the past five years and to map out future research opportunities in university human resource management using a systematic literature review approach. The literature review findings indicate a positive correlation between GHRM practices and pro-environmental behavior among university employees, mainly when supported by GHRM training and education programs. However, there still needs to be more research that presents opportunities for future exploration. This study contributes to the theoretical framework of sustainability and human resource management. © The Authors.

Authors: Hujjatullah Fazlurrahman; Udisubakti Ciptomulyono; Irhamah
Title: Volatility Technology and Environmental Sustainability to Improve Green Innovation in Small Medium Enterprises: A Bibliometric Analysis
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: This analysis Bibliometric Study examines the intersection of volatility, technology, and environmental sustainability in enhancing green innovation among Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs). The review aims to identify key trends, challenges, and strategies that enable SMEs to adopt green innovation practices effectively. By analyzing a range of scholarly articles, this study provides a comprehensive understanding of how SMEs can leverage technological advancements and navigate environmental volatility to achieve sustainable growth. The review finds that market and environmental volatility can compel SMEs to adopt new technologies and sustainable practices for business continuity. Conversely, investments in green technologies and effective environmental management can help SMEs manage volatility better. Collaboration between SMEs, research institutions, and policymakers is crucial in creating an enabling environment for green innovation in SMEs. The review highlights the need for a holistic approach integrating technological, organizational, and policy-level interventions to support SMEs' transition towards environmental sustainability and long-term competitiveness. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: environmental; green innovation; small medium enterprises; sustainability; technology

Authors: Hujjatullah Fazlurrahman; Bima Setyo Nugroho; Maryam Jamilah Asha'ari; Mazzlida Mat Deli; Wan Noordiana Wan Hanafi; Salina Binti Daud
Title: E-Recruitment with AI in GHRM for Corporate University Sustainability to Improve Organization Performance: Systematic Literature Review
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: This comprehensive and systematic literature review meticulously examines the intricate fusion and integration of various electronic recruitment methodologies, which are increasingly being utilized in conjunction with advanced artificial intelligence (AI) technologies, all within the expansive framework of global human resource management (GHRM), with the overarching objective of promoting and fostering sustainability within corporate universities. The review aims to understand how these technologies impact organizational performance and sustainability initiatives. It investigates how these digital tools enable organizations to streamline recruitment processes, enhance candidate screening and selection, and promote diversity and inclusion in the global talent pool. The review also explores the role of e-recruitment and AI in supporting corporate university programs, which are critical for developing a skilled and sustainable workforce. Additionally, the review highlights how corporate universities can leverage e-recruitment and AI to identify, nurture, and retain top talent, thereby contributing to the long-term sustainability of the organization. The insights gathered from this systematic literature review provide a comprehensive understanding of the opportunities and challenges associated with the intersection of e-recruitment, AI, and GHRM in the context of corporate university sustainability. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: artificial intelligent; corporate university sustainability; e-recruitment; green human resource management; organization

Authors: Vira Amalia Putri; Inas Nurfadia Futri; Tastaftiyan Risfandy; Reniati Reniati; Hujjatullah Fazlurrahman
Title: How Does Fintech Lending Affect Islamic Local Banks' Efficiency During COVID-19 Pandemic in Indonesia?
Journal: International Journal of Economics and Management
Abstract: Fintech lending, also known as peer-to-peer (P2P) lending or online loans, refers to lending provided by non-bank financial technology-based companies that provide financial services by connecting lenders and borrowers on a specific online platform. This paper investigates the impact of fintech lending development on the efficiency of Islamic local banks in Indonesia. Local banks are chosen because they differ from national commercial banks in terms of their characteristics and products, and they also serve limited customers in a specific local area (i.e., provincial level). The presence of Islamic local banks in Indonesia is unique because it fills the gap and has a significant contribution to Muslims who do not have (or do not need) access to larger Islamic commercial banks. This is particularly noteworthy given that Indonesia is the most populous Muslim country. Using a sample of 161 Islamic local banks in Indonesia and provincial-level fintech lending data from 2020Q1 to 2020Q4, we find that fintech development in Indonesia erodes Islamic local banks’ efficiency, suggesting that their presence is significant and could also be damaging for local banks. However, our finding reveals that the negative effects of fintech development can be reduced in banks with higher levels of efficiency. Our results call for policymakers to monitor the development of fintech to maintain a lending ecosystem that can ultimately relate to financial service stability. © International Journal of Economics and Management.
Keywords: data envelopment analysis; efficiency; Fintech; Islamic local banks

Authors: Tias Andarini Indarwati; Anik Lestari Andjarwati; Riska Dhenabayu; Sanaji
Title: Strengthening Adaptive Capability and Networking Capability to Enhance Innovation and Business Performance of Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) in Indonesia
Journal: 2024 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications
Abstract: Small and Medium Businesses (SMEs) in Indonesia have been significantly impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic, especially in Sidoarjo Regency. The purpose of this study is to look into how networking and adaptive capabilities can help SMEs innovate and perform better as a business. Data were collected from 100 SMEs using purposive sampling with a quantitative approach. This study's data analysis method was structural equation modeling (SEM) based on partial least squares (PLS). The findings indicate that adaptive capability has an indirect impact on business performance through innovation, emphasizing the essential role of innovative practices. Networking capability, on the other hand, directly and indirectly impacts performance by facilitating resource exchange, reducing transaction costs, and enhancing supply chain reliability. This study highlights the critical importance of developing adaptive and networking capabilities to drive innovation and sustain performance. It also provides insights into how small and medium enterprises (SMEs) can act strategically in navigating dynamic environments to maintain their competitive advantage and resilience.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: Adaptive capability; innovation; networking capability; performance

Authors: Agung Prijo Budijono; Gigih Siantoro; Riska Dhenabayu; Setiyo Hartoto; Donny Ardy Kusuma; Awang Firmansyah; Muhammad Reza Aziz Prasetya; Jose V. Garcia-Jimenez
Title: Determining the Correlation of Fat-Free Mass, Power, and Maximum Hamstring Force in Female Basketball Athletes
Journal: Physical Education Theory and Methodology
Abstract: Background. In competitive sports, especially basketball, physical attributes such as agility, speed, strength, and power are crucial for performance. Objectives. This study aimed to examine the correlation between fat-free mass (FFM), countermovement jump (CMJ) power, and maximum hamstring force in female basketball athletes, providing insights that could enhance performance and reduce injury risk. Materials and methods. This observational cross-sectional study investigates the relationship between fat-free mass (FFM), power, and maximum hamstring force in 45 female basketball athletes. The Anderson-Darling test for assessing normality and Spearman’s correlation for non-normally distributed data were used to conduct the study. Results. Significant correlations were found between FFM and maximum hamstring force in the both right (r = 0.585, p < 0.001) and left legs (r = 0.522, p < 0.001), as well as between (CMJ) power and maximum hamstring force for both legs (right: r = 0.467, p = 0.001; left: r = 0.434, p = 0.003). Additionally, power was significantly correlated with jump height (r = 0.604, p < 0.001). Conclusions. These findings suggest that FFM and power are critical determinants of hamstring strength and explosive performance in female basketball athletes. © Budijono, A. P., Siantoro, G., Dhenabayu, R., Hartoto, S., Kusuma, D. A., Firmansyah, A., Prasetya, M. R. A., & Garcia-Jimenez, J. V., 2024.
Keywords: correlation; fat-free mass; female basketball athletes; hamstring force; power
TLDR: Findings suggest that FFM and power are critical determinants of hamstring strength and explosive performance in female basketball athletes.

Authors: Anita Safitri; Muhammad Fajar Wahyudi Rahman; Anissa Esadora Hartanto; Moch. Nuriko Firdaus
Title: Implementation of Web-Based Metaverse Virtual Classes Integration in Academic Education
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: Integrating web-based virtual classes in university settings within the metaverse represents a transformative approach to education, offering immersive and interactive learning experiences. This research aims to explore the practical application of this innovative method in Indonesian education while identifying any discrepancies with existing theories. By employing usability testing, the study seeks to provide an analysis of the implementation of web-based virtual classes in university. Usability testing in this context can help identify usability issues and improve the overall user experience of the learning system. Using usability testing to provide valuable insights from users, including students and educators, regarding the functionality and user-friendliness of the virtual learning environment. The primary objective of this research is to bridge the gap between theoretical frameworks and practical implementation of web-based virtual classes in Indonesian university using usability testing based on Jakob Nielsen's theory which has never been used in this case in the previous studies. Previous studies found that the application of metaverse is effective in education, but others explain practically still have any lacks. By examining the actual application of this technology, researchers could maximize the aim to enhance teaching and learning practices. From this research, we found out that, web-based virtual classes are usable for learning activities and could help pave the way for more tailored and effective educational strategies in the metaverse. Through this study, we found what aspects we need to consider to develop a web-based metaverse virtual class. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: human computer interaction; metaverse; university; usability testing; virtual class

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; Fathimah Az Zahrah; Dendra Aptanata Nugraha; Prayudi Setiawan Prabowo; Anita Safitri; Puguh Jayadi
Title: Predicting Software Size Based on Conceptual Data Model (Case Study: Shrimp Pond System Management)
Journal: ICECOS 2024 - 4th International Conference on Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
Abstract: Farming application requires precise software size estimation. This work suggests a combination technique employing the database complexity index (DBCI) and COSMIC Functional Size Measurement (COSMIC FSM) to assess the size of a prawn farming management system, whereas existing methods frequently ignore data complexity. There are four steps in the suggested method: (1) determining the real effort; (2) utilising DBCI to calculate database complexity; (3) utilising COSMIC FSM to calculate data movement complexity; and (4) assessing the amount of estimated size toward actual size. The findings of this research is 57.5 percent deviation between actual effort and estimated size using DBCI and COSMIC FSM. This study emphasises how crucial it is to take data complexity into account when estimating the size of software, especially for systems that largely rely on data, like shrimp ponds management systems. The suggested technique offers a more thorough and precise means of calculating programme size, which helps to improve project planning, resource allocation, and overall software development efficiency. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: COSMIC; pond application; software estimation; software size

Authors: Hafid Kholidi Hadi; Mohammad Isa Irawan; Reny Nadlifatin; Maela Madel L. Cahigas; Anita Safitri
Title: Trend and research the use of virtual reality to increase student's negotiation skills in realizing Sustainability
Journal: E3S Web of Conferences
Abstract: This study aims to investigate the relationship between the utilisation of virtual reality technology (VR) in educational contexts and students' perceptions of their capabilities. This research employs a quantitative methodology utilising Biblioshiny, which was conducted through a comprehensive literature review of 644 papers discussing virtual reality and self-efficacy in the field of education from Scopus. The search results were refined using Biblioshiny, which yielded 50 papers deemed pertinent to this study. The results of the data analysis indicate a significant relationship between the use of virtual reality in learning and the improvement of students' self-efficacy, which aligns with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) mission number 4, 'Quality Education', and number 9, 'Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure'. These findings offer new insights into the potential of virtual reality as a learning support tool, which can enhance students' confidence and motivation to learn. The paper also considers the implications of these findings for further research and learning practice. © The Authors.

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; Ivory Inggri Mahadewi; Ayu Febrini Putri; Najmi Khairunnisa Gunawan; Tria Rizki Rosmalia; Adillah Rodiah; Aura Diva Nadira; Hafizah Aryani
Title: Involving Data Complexity in Software Size Estimation Based on Conceptual Data Model (Case Study: Catering Management Information System)
Journal: ICECOS 2024 - 4th International Conference on Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
Abstract: This research focuses on software size estimation, which often ignores aspects of data complexity. Software estimation generally only considers the difficulty of source code development and the experience of the development team. Ideally, software development should consider the difficulty of source code development and data in a balanced way. However, in practice, data is often considered an integral part of the system so it is included in the size estimation. This research uses a combination method between COSMIC Function Point Analysis (CFP) and Database Complexity Index (DBCI). The combination of CFP and DBCI is used as the main method to estimate the size of the Catering Management Information System. This research consists of four main stages: (1) actual business calculation, (2) database complexity calculation using DBCI, (3) data movement complexity calculation using COSMIC FSM, and (4) calculation of the estimated size. The results show that the data complexity is 32, while the database complexity is 221. The comparison between the estimated software size and the actual effort shows a difference of 31.68 percent. The limitation of this research is the absence of source code calculation or other methods, so the comparison between estimation and actual effort becomes unstable. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: COSMIC; data complexity; database complexity index; software size estimation

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; I.G.B. Prabu Vedayana Priharta; Zidane Al-Munir; Muhammad Rafi Rizqi; Hisbulloh Edgaritoh; Rayhan Aimar Narawi; Muhammad Rizal Maulana; Wildan Arsthasya Putra Budi; Citra Nurhayati
Title: Calculating Software Size Based on Conceptual Data Model Using Data Complexities of Fishermen Information System
Journal: ICECOS 2024 - 4th International Conference on Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
Abstract: In research on the calculation of software size estimation, it often ignored the data complexity factor. Ideally, a software is developed in tandem between source code and database. However, in reality in software development, data is considered a complementary unit that cannot be separated from the system so that the existence of data is included in the size calculation. In this research, the method used is a combination of the Common Software Measurement International Consortium (COSMIC) functional size measurement with the database complexity index (DBCI). There are 4 stages in this research, including: (1) actual business calculation, (2) database complexity calculation using DBCI, (3) data movement complexity calculation using COSMIC and (4) estimation of the size of the fisherman information system. The result of this research is the amount of data movement complexity based on COSMIC is 129, while the database complexity is 349. The comparison between the estimated size of the software and the actual effort is 170.8 percent. The limitation of this research is that there is no source code calculation or other methods, so the comparison results between the estimate and the actual effort are fluctuating. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: Calculation; COSMIC; Database complexity; Software size; System

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; Yogantara Setya Dharmawan
Title: A Proposed Model for Embedding Risk Proportion in Software Development Effort Estimation
Journal: Procedia Computer Science
Abstract: The risk of project implementation failure has always been a primary concern for software development team managers. Previous studies have recommended that risk should be considered as a significant parameter in software development effort estimation. The aim of this research is to create a modelling of risk proportion in software development projects based on the risk classification from previous studies. The analysis method used in this research is quantitative, based on the observation of the risk proportion values against the actual effort. Seventeen software projects were analysed to derive the risk proportion model. After the experimental process of calculating risk occurrence, the accuracy of the risk proportion in relation to the real effort in the 17 projects was found to be 91.97%. This risk proportion model is then incorporated as an additional formulation in estimating the economics of software development projects using commonly encountered methods, especially those related to non-technical or environmental complexity factors. In future research, the integration of risk proportion with complexity factor data will be pursued. © 2023 The Authors. Published by Elsevier B.V.
Keywords: economic development; risk proportion; Software estimation; software risk; use case points

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; Riyanarto Sarno; Endang Siti Astuti
Title: Machine Learning Implementation in Use Case Points Method: A 30-year Perspective
Journal: 2024 International Conference on Information Technology Systems and Innovation
Abstract: Machine learning is an advanced technology in this era. In its development, accurate calculations related to complexity must be carefully considered. One of the methods used is the use case points (UCP) calculation model. By using literature review and bibliometric tool, the results of using this model in machine learning development can be analyzed. It can be concluded that this calculation model is very useful in assessing the complexity of machine learning. However, there are still shortcomings that need to be refined in its usage. Therefore, further step and development are required, as seen in the design of deep learning technology. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: implementation; literature review; machine learning; use case points

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; Willyam Dominick Soegiarto; Kevin Fero Fernando; Givvara Reihan; Meuthya Kayla Puspita; Muhammad Adam Al Ghazali; Shafarani Putri Raissa; Khodijah; Citra Nurhayati
Title: Software Size Measurement Using Data Complexities (Case Study: Marketing Kit Monitoring System)
Journal: International Conference on Electrical Engineering
Abstract: Software size estimation calculations often do not consider data complexity. However, in practice, data is considered an integral part of the system, so it needs to be taken into account in software size estimation. The combination of Common Software Measurement International Consortium (COSMIC) Functional Size Measurement and Database Complexity Index (DBCI) is the main method in estimating the size of marketing tool monitoring system. This research involves 4 stages, which include: (1) actual business calculation, (2) database complexity calculation using DBCI, (3) data movement complexity calculation using COSMIC FSM, and (4) marketing tool monitoring system size estimation. The findings of this study are that the level of data movement complexity based on COSMIC FSM is 78, while the database complexity is 236. The comparison between the results of software size estimation and actual effort is as follows 141.54 percent. A limitation of this study is the absence of source code calculations or other methods, which causes fluctuations in the comparison between estimated and actual effort.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: COSMIC; data complexity; DBCI; marketing monitoring system; software estimation

Authors: Renny Sari Dewi; Thessa Karinda Araynawa; Futari Maysya Prasanna; Nadia Felianasari; Rizki Rahmawati; Annisa Esadora Hartantc; Lathufa Syafa Kamilah; Al-Kairana Mazaya
Title: Improving Software Size Estimation Using Data Complexity (Case Study: Research and Community Service Monitoring Apps)
Journal: International Conference on Electrical Engineering
Abstract: The Research and Community Service Monitoring Apps is an essential application for managing and evaluating research and community service activities. In research related to software size estimation, the complexity of data is often disregarded. Nevertheless, in the realm of software development, data is regarded as an integral complement to the system, thus its presence is inherently factored into size computations. This study employs a hybrid approach, combining the Common Software Measurement International Consortium (COSMIC) functional size measurement with the database complexity index (DBCI). With a focus on the 'Research and Community Service Monitoring Apps,' the research delineates four essential stages: (1) the calculation of actual effort, (2) determining data movement complexity through COSMIC FSM, (3) assessing database complexity using DBCI, and (4) estimating the system's size. The outcomes reveal the data movement complexity based on COSMIC FSM to be 80, while the database complexity is 196. The comparison between estimated software size and actual effort expended yields 43.75%. However, the study is limited by the absence of source code calculation or alternative methods, leading to fluctuating comparisons between estimation and actual effort.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: complexity; database; monitoring; research; software estimation

Authors: Hafid Kholidi Hadi; Ika Diyah Candra Arifah; Azmil Chusnaini; Nadia Nur Thahirrah; Archam Zahidin; Moch. Nuriko Firdaus
Title: A Bibliometric Analysis: Challenges and Opportunities of Implementing Time Management Technology for Flexible Work in the Service Sector
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: The service sector has witnessed a surge in flexible work arrangements, prompting the exploration of time management technology's impact on efficiency and adaptable work environments. This research employs a bibliometric analysis with a qualitative approach through literature review. Data will be sourced from the Scopus database and analyzed using bibliometric mapping and content analysis to gain a comprehensive understanding of how time management technology influences time flexibility in the service sector. This study aims to identify emerging opportunities and challenges, focusing on the gap between existing research and the specific needs of IT-enabled business process reengineering (BPR). The analysis will explore how technology can optimize flexible work practices without compromising employee well-being and organizational integrity. By integrating insights from bibliometric analysis and recommendations for optimal technology use, this study will contribute valuable knowledge for organizations seeking to leverage technology for improved time management in a flexible work environment. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: service sector; time flexibility; time management technology

Authors: Hafid Kholidi Hadi; Mohammad Isa Irawan; Reny Nadlifatin
Title: The Relationship of Virtual Reality and Interpersonal Communication: Bibliometric Overview
Journal: Procedia Computer Science
Abstract: Virtual reality (VR) is a technology that allows users to interact with a simulated environment. VR has been used in a variety of fields, including education, training, and entertainment. Recent research has shown that VR can also be used to improve interpersonal communication and self-efficacy. This study intends to highlight the expansion of knowledge and the dissemination of research in the field of virtual reality and its influence on interpersonal communication. For this purpose, the research articles published in the Scopus database from 1993 to 2023, covering 30 years, have been taken to perform a bibliometric analysis. These studies suggest that VR can be a valuable tool for improving interpersonal communication and self-efficacy. VR can provide a safe and controlled environment for people to practice their skills. VR can also provide feedback and support that can help people improve their performance. As VR technology continues to develop, it is likely that VR will be used more and more to improve interpersonal communication and self-efficacy. © 2023 The Authors. Published by Elsevier B.V.
Keywords: Interpersonal communication; Self-efficacy; Virtual; Virtual reality

Authors: Muhammad Fajar Wahyudi Rahman; Anang Kistyanto; Ali Roziqin; Ahmad Ajib Ridlwan; Febrika Yogie Hermanto
Title: Resilience capital roadmap during and post COVID-19: A recovery-based framework
Journal: Strategies and Frameworks for Relearning in Organizations
Abstract: The aims of this chapter are to systematically review the development of the literature on a roadmap framework based on resilience and the recovery of MSMEs from the crisis that emerged due to the COVID-19 pandemic. In addition, this research is used to identify research topics that must be carried out and research opportunities in the future. A systematic literature review was performed, analyzing studies related to COVID-19, MSMEs or SMEs, and resilience. The authors propose a resilience roadmap during the crisis with a recovery-based framework for reviving the MSMEs post COVID-19. The roadmap outlines six prominent resources or clusters for building resilience processes: health capital, psychological capital, human capital, social capital, technological capital, and economics capital through six applications of resilience capital and grouped into two strategies recovery-based framework. They argue that by using a resilience roadmap with a recovery-based framework that is inclusive, the MSMEs may transform into a new global economy with make policies to respond to the crisis. © 2025, IGI Global. All rights reserved.

Authors: Sunu Widianto; Haider Muhammad Abdul Sahib; Muhammad Fajar Wahyudi Rahman
Title: Task Interdependence, Team Identity and Team Performance: A Bottom-Up Multilevel Model
Journal: SAGE Open
Abstract: Most research on social identity theory has been separately examined either at the team or individual levels. This paper aims to expand the use of the social identity theory and illuminates an emerging mechanism of task interdependence at the individual level that can affect the nature of the team identity by testing how team power distance act as a moderator Iraqi universities, this study employs multilevel structural equation modeling (MSEM) with Mplus 7.2 to test the model. The results reveal a positive indirect relationship between individual-level task interdependence and team performance through team identity. In addition, team power distance does not moderate a positive relationship between individual-level task interdependence and team identity. This study illuminates the link between task interdependence and team identity, which can eventually occur through a bottom-up mechanism to obtain high team performance. This study is novel for several reasons. First, this study finds a bottom-up mechanism between task interdependence at the individual level to team performance through team identity. Second, this study offers new empirical findings that, in the context of a middle east culture known as the high level of power distance, team power distance (i.e., contextual variable) does not associate with the interdependence of an individual to perform tasks and team identity. In addition, this answers the research call to investigate the dynamic interactions between individuals in teams and team-based overall, which has not been discussed in previous studies. © The Author(s) 2024.
Keywords: higher education; micro-macro effects; task interdependence; team identity; team performance; team power distance

Authors: Shavilla Auliansyah; Salwa Audrey Fidelia Purnomo; Arrahma Carine Suwarno; Muhammad Fajar Wahyudi Rahman
Title: Gamification as a Practice of Performance Improvement: The Role of Innovative Behavior
Journal: 2024 International Conference on Decision Aid Sciences and Applications
Abstract: Gamification has generated a lot of attention and controversy in organizations as many see it as a powerful tool to motivate individuals and increase activity in business. Not much research has been carried out in the context of gamification applied to the automotive sector in Indonesia. This research aims to try to understand the influence of gamification strategies on employee performance through individual innovative behavior among Mitsubishi dealer employees in Indonesia. A quantitative approach was chosen and applied in this study. Partial Least Square - Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) was applied as a data analysis technique supported by the Smart-PLS 4 computer software program with a sample size of 120 respondents. The findings of this study indicate that the gamification program has not been able to fully have a positive impact on employee performance, in this case the level of individual employee innovative behavior plays a full mediation role in making gamification in the workplace have a positive impact on improving employee performance. The novelty of this research is that it explores one specific outcome of gamified competition based on affordance theory. It analyzes the role of innovative behavior as a mediating factor and expands the scope of gamification literature by focusing on a workplace context driven by workplace design that incorporates competition through gamification.  © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: Automotive Sector; Employee Performance; Gamification; Indonesia; Innovative Behavior

Authors: Kai Chen Goh; Tonni Agustiono Kurniawan; Nadzirah Zainordin; Ika Diyah Candra Arifah; Muhamad Azahar Abas; Md Asrul Nasid Masrom; Sulzakimin Mohamed; Roshartini Omar; Sui Lai Khoo; Hun Chuen Gui; Tien Choon Toh; Choo Wou Onn
Title: Expediting decarbonization in energy, waste, and water sector through digitalization in sustainable smart cities (SSC): Case-studies in Malaysia and China based on Industry 5.0 paradigm
Journal: Sustainable Cities and Society
Abstract: This work explores the application of Industry 5.0 principles in smart cities development in Malaysia and China, focusing on digital transformation for sustainable urban development. The study presents case-studies from both the countries, highlighting the implementation strategies, challenges, and outcomes associated with integrating advanced technologies to enhance efficiency, climate resilience, and sustainability. This study aims to develop a data-driven methodology to address the absence of region-specific frameworks for sustainable smart cities (SSCs) and to evaluate their impacts. In Malaysia, the implementation of smart energy management systems that utilize IoT and AI has shown promise in reducing carbon footprints and maximizing resource efficiency. China's focus on smart water management using sensor networks and real-time data analytics provides insights into effective water conservation. Smart waste management systems have increased recycling rates by 20–30 %. Progress is crucial for the region's pursuit of SSCs to reach a global investment of USD 2.5 trillion by 2025. This work concludes by discussing the implications of this work in both countries to achieve sustainable urbanization through Industry 5.0 technologies. This work offers recommendations for policymakers, urban planners, and technologists to navigate the complexities of smart city development, while providing a roadmap to leverage digital transformation to achieve decarbonization goals in energy, waste and water sector by 2060. © 2024 Elsevier Ltd
Keywords: Carbon neutrality; Cities; Climate action; Net zero emission; Sustainability

Authors: Ika Diyah Candra Arifah; Arya Wanda Wirayuda
Title: Consumer Attitude and Purchase Behaviour Toward Artificial Intelligence-Based Features in Smart Hotel Mobile Apps
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: The influence of a smart hotel mobile app on customer acquisition can be significant, as it can enhance the overall consumer experience and provide unique value propositions that attract and retain customers. Developing a smart hotel app involves several key steps to ensure that the app meets the needs of both the hotel management and the guests. A well-designed and feature-rich smart hotel app can play a crucial role in customer acquisition by offering convenience, personalization, communication, and value-added services that enhance the overall guest experience and differentiate the hotel from competitors. With a quantitative approach, this research aims to determine the effect of AI features in smart hotel mobile apps on consumer attitude (user experience evaluation, technology user-friendliness, and information clarity) and customer purchase behaviour. This research will help hoteliers and developers design and optimize smart apps to meet their target audience's needs and expectations effectively. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: AI feature; consumer attitude; consumer purchase behaviour; smart hotel and tourism app

Authors: Ika Diyah Candra Arifah
Title: Transforming Modern Marketing: Artificial Intelligence Implementation for SEO Marketing of Online Store in E-Commerce Marketplace
Journal: 2024 12th International Conference on Cyber and IT Service Management
Abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) in e-commerce apps allows businesses to personalize the shopping experience for their customers. AI can recommend relevant products, create personalized product descriptions, and generate realistic product images by analyzing customer data and shopping patterns. With the help of AI, e-commerce apps can provide targeted recommendations based on customers' preferences and browsing history. This research focuses on a case study of AI implementation in Natural Language Generation (NLG), Image Generation, Chatbots and Virtual Assistants, Recommendation Systems, and Price Optimization as part of SEO Marketing in e-commerce apps. With a case study research approach in one of the global e-commerce marketplace apps, it is hoped that this research can provide thorough research on AI in e-commerce apps and valuable insights into its potential applications and implications for achieving digital marketing goals for e-commerce apps. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: apps; artificial intelligence; e-commerce; SEO marketing

Authors: Septian Rahman Hakim; Aris Nasuha; Dessy Irmawati; Moh Alif Hidayat Sofyan; Purno Tri Aji; Ahmad Taufiq Musaddid; Indra Hidayatulloh; Dodik Arwin Dermawan
Title: Trends and research on heart detection in using yolo algorithm to realize the 3rd SDG
Journal: E3S Web of Conferences
Abstract: The purpose of this study is to assess the role, trends, and developments of the YOLO algorithm for healthy heart detection in advancing the 3rd SDGs. The research methodology used involved bibliometric analysis using VOSviewer and Biblioshiny tools. VOSviewer and Biblioshiny were used to examine the distribution of annual documents across countries, institutions, journals, authors, and interrelationships between keywords. The results show that Indonesia is in the 5th position of contributing countries among 10 countries/regions involved in the development of the YOLO algorithm for healthy heart detection. The data used in this research is sourced from the Scopus database in .csv format. In addition to data extraction from the Scopus database, this research also investigates the implementation of the YOLO algorithm for healthy heart detection using data from the Automated Cardiac Diagnosis Challenge (ACDC). © The Authors.
TLDR: The results show that Indonesia is in the 5th position of contributing countries among 10 countries/regions involved in the development of the YOLO algorithm for healthy heart detection.

Authors: A. Susanti; W. Rosdiana; D.A. Dermawan; A. Widayanti; A. Muhtadi
Title: Development of Gresik – Mojokerto Railway Infrastructure to Accelerate Economic Growth in Gerbangkertosusila
Journal: IOP Conference Series: Earth and Environmental Science
Abstract: The most important economic area in East Java is the Gerbangkertosusila (GKS) area. The GKS area between Gresik – Mojokerto is not yet connected directly, almost all goods have to go through Surabaya and Sidoarjo first. This results in high logistics costs and long travel times. The research method used in this research is a qualitative research method on economic growth factors in the GKS area. The factors considered in economic growth are population, number of people of productive age, number of people who are not yet working, minimum income/month, number of poorer people, percentage of poorer people, length of roads, number of people accessing the internet, number of telecommunication towers, length of roads damaged, investment value, export value, import value, and gross regional domestic product (GRDP). The analytical tool used in the research is a descriptive method to measure and assess the quality of economic growth and changes in regional economic growth factors, assisting strategic planning for the construction of new railway lines as the right solution for developing regional economic growth. Therefore, to accelerate economic growth and reduce logistics costsin the GKS area, a new railway line between Gresik - Mojokerto should be built. If logistics costs are low, it will reduce the price of goods and increase the opportunity to order goods and open up new jobs. If new job opportunities are opened, it will be possible to reduce the number of unemployed people by 375,392 people and the poorer population by 719,944 people. So it is hoped that it will increase economic growth to reach 7%. Only the Gresik and Sidoarjo regions have met this economic growth. Mojokerto's economic growth is still 5.56% and lags behind other regions. If the construction of the new Gresik – Mojokerto train line is realized, then economic growth in the GKS region of at least 7% will not be difficult to achieve. © 2024 Institute of Physics Publishing. All rights reserved.

Authors: Novia Restu Windayani; Hirnanda Dimas Pradana; Dodik Arwin Dermawan; OnnyFrasinanta Anggara; Diah Anggraeny; Acep Ovel Novari Beny
Title: Montal app development: Mental monitoring for disability in adapting to the community environment
Journal: E3S Web of Conferences
Abstract: Technological developments can be used for a wide variety of benefits One of them is the use of assessment for students or someone independent of the community environment. This monitoring activity is not only carried out by the concerned, but also involves the homeroom teacher, teacher council, and parents/guardians Learners. The goal is that this activity is not only carried out in schools, in Parents also contribute to shaping the disciplinary character of their children. It is associated with people with disabilities and the importance of the role of the environment is enormous in fostering the mental health of people with disabilities. Good surroundings can be a critical success factor in mental health growth, because can facilitate people with disabilities to blend in and socialize. Montal's application is carried out with media feasibility tests before being used by people with disabilities. Declared Fit for use with a number of 3.59 In the implementation of the MONTAL research in a trial with a total of 20 SLB Gedangan Students and 15 UNESA Students with disabilities getting results of 3.05 it was stated that this application could be suitable for special assessment of students or students with disabilities to be confident in the surrounding environment. © The Authors, published by EDP Sciences.

Authors: Nadi Suprapto; Septian Rahman Hakim; Hasan Nuurul Hidaayatullaah; Dodik Arwin Dermawan; Dimas Novian Aditia Syahputra
Title: A Bibliometric Analysis of Virtual Reality Research on Critical Thinking Skills and Student Responses using PLS-SEM
Journal: ACM International Conference Proceeding Series
Abstract: Incorporating virtual reality technology is one way to enhance critical thinking skills in education. However, only a few studies have looked into the effect of virtual reality on student responses and the factors that influence critical thinking skills. To understand how students learn through virtual reality, this study analyzed the relationship between critical thinking skills and virtual reality research on student responses. The study analyzed trends in virtual reality in science learning from the Scopus database using bibliometric analysis. The result indicates that research in VR-CTS has significantly increased over the last decade. The United States has the highest number of publications, followed by China and Indonesia. China is also the most cited country in VR-CTS research. The most productive researcher is Rusyati L., and the Journal of Physics Conference Series is the most relevant source. Visualization mapping shows a strong relationship between VR and critical thinking skills, particularly for students in learning. Thematic evolution analysis indicates that VR is widely applied in education, training, and safety beyond entertainment. These findings underscore the increasing significance of VR in education, particularly its profound impact on student motivation and critical thinking skills. This impact is supported by the heightened research activity and positive student responses observed in this bibliometric analysis. The novelty of this study lies in its exploration of global dynamics in VR-CTS. Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) confirms that virtual reality exerts a significant influence on student responses, demonstrating its potential to analyze the complex relationship between VR-CTS and student outcomes. © 2024 Copyright held by the owner/author(s).
Keywords: Bibliometric; Critical Thinkings Skills; PLS-SEM; Student Response'; Virtual Reality
TLDR: Analysis of trends in virtual reality in science learning from the Scopus database using bibliometric analysis indicates that research in VR-CTS has significantly increased over the last decade, and Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling confirms that virtual reality exerts a significant influence on student responses.

Authors: Salamun Rohman Nudin; Rahmat Gernowo; Maman Somantri; Adi Wibowo
Title: Multi Task Classification Using Deep Learning Approaches for Big Five Personality Traits Prediction: A Review
Journal: Proceedings - 11th International Conference on Information Technology
Abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive review of deep learning methods for the Big Five personality traits prediction using multi-task classification. The purpose of the review is to determine the performance of models developed in previous research to describe research opportunities and challenges in the future. The Big Five model is a framework in psychology that categorizes human personality into five dimensions: openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, and neuroticism. The review begins by analyzing 22 articles from the Science Direct, IEEE Xplore, and Scopus databases by applying screening using the Big Five Personality keywords. Then study the concepts of deep learning for model development such as: input, architecture, method, dataset, and performance to identify the strengths and limitations of each approach. The review results of the highest average accuracy by modality such as unimodal text with essay dataset (80.98%), unimodal image with ImageNet dataset (95.36%), unimodal audio with SSPNet dataset (79.48%), bimodal image and audio with Chalearn Video First Impression dataset (91.67%), multimodal text, image, and audio with Chalearn Video First Impression dataset (91.88%). The average accuracy of the Big Five Personality traits was highest (95.36%) and lowest (59%). This highlights the need for standard benchmarks, interpretable models and interdisciplinary collaboration to improve the accuracy, reliability and ethical implications of personality prediction. Future research is expected to pave the way for advancements in understanding human personality through deep learning approaches using other modalities such as EEG, GSR, and PPG. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: big five personality; deep learning; IEEE Xplore; science direct; scopus

Authors: Tri Rijanto; B. Santhosh Kumar; Aws Zuhair Sameen; Takveer Singh; Suruchi Pimple; Swati M. Patil
Title: Exploring Advanced Techniques in Multilevel Fusion Score Level for Enhanced Data Integration in Complex Systems
Journal: Fusion: Practice and Applications
Abstract: We have discovered five novel strategies to enhance data fusion in complex systems. This page provides a comprehensive explanation of these five methodologies. Data may be combined with a list. Examples of techniques include entropy-based data selection and parameter optimization for data fusion. This technique effectively resolves all problems related to merging records. Accurate, rapid, and easily expandable. Ablation studies assess the effectiveness of various techniques. Every process is crucial; omitting anyone would adversely affect the mix. This approach may integrate data from several sources to guarantee accuracy and utility. This facilitates the use of intricate technologies, hence enhancing data integration. The study promotes further inquiry and implementation. These results indicate that using this method might enhance the process of combining data. © 2024, American Scientific Publishing Group (ASPG). All rights reserved.
Keywords: Anomaly Detection; Data Integration; Data Scalability; Entropy-Based Selection; Fusion Algorithms; Multilevel Integration; Parameter Optimization; Precision; Robustness
TLDR: Results indicate that using this method might enhance the process of combining data, and facilitates the use of intricate technologies, hence enhancing data integration.

Authors: Widi Aribowo; Heri Suryoatmojo; Feby Agung Pamuji
Title: A NOVEL MODIFIED OSPREY OPTIMIZATION ALGORITHM FOR TUNING DROOP CONTROL PARAMETERS IN DC MICROGRID
Journal: International Journal of Innovative Computing
Abstract: The hybrid approach presented in this study combines the Osprey Optimization Algorithm (OOA) with Lévy flight optimization. It is possible to mimic an osprey’s natural behavior by using the Osprey Optimization Algorithm (OOA) approach. This hunting approach is used by ospreys to locate their prey, hunt it, and then position it so that it may be consumed. The stride length during a Lévy’s flight, a specific kind of random walk, can be described by a heavy-tailed probability distribution. OOA is the primary algorithm of the suggested approach, which is coupled with the Lévy flight optimization technique. We refer to this method as MOOA. The suggested approach is used for droop control’s Proportional-Integral (PI) secondary control optimization. This article compares the Aquila Optimizer (AO), Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA), Marine Predator Algorithm (MPA), Golden Jackal Optimization Algorithm (GJO), and Reptile Search Algorithm (RSA) to assess the efficacy of the suggested approach. Performance tests on droop control are compared with convergence curves in this test. The simulation results show that the proposed method performs strongly and promisingly when applied to secondary control in droop control. © ICIC International 2024.
Keywords: Control system; DC microgrids; Droop control; Metaheuristic; Osprey optimization algorithm

Authors: Widi Aribowo; Heri Suryoatmojo; Feby Agung Pamuji
Title: Improved Droop Control Based on Modified Osprey Optimization Algorithm in DC Microgrid
Journal: Journal of Robotics and Control (JRC)
Abstract: In this research, a modified Osprey optimization algorithm (MOOA) is presented to optimize droop control parameters. MOOA is a modification of the Osprey optimization algorithm by adding levy flight which has the advantage of exploiting a wider space and being adaptive to environmental changes. This research also modifies droop control, Proportional Integral Derivative (PID) is applied to secondary control. PID has flexibility in responding to changes in system conditions and fast response in dealing with system changes. The PID parameters are optimized using MOOA and are called MOOA-PID. The MOOA method is validated using 23 CEC2017 benchmarks-function and performance on DC microgrid systems. This research uses the latest algorithms as a comparison, namely One-to-One Based Optimizer (OOBO), Preschool Educational Optimization Algorithm (PEOA), and the red-tailed hawk (RTH) algorithm in testing 23 CEC2017 benchmark functions. From the simulation of the 23 CEC2017 benchmark function, it is known that the MOOA method has better capabilities. MOOA has advantages in 15 out of 23 benchmark functions. In DC microgrid system testing, MOOA-PID is compared with the Proportional Integral (PI) method which is optimized with MOOA and is called MOOA-PI. Testing on the microgrid is aimed at determining the performance of the transient response of power, voltage and current in the system. Tests on DC microgrid systems found that the application of MOOA-PID in secondary control had better capabilities than MOOA-PI. The average value of voltage overshoot from MOOA-PID is 9.828% better than MOOA-PI. The average ITSE MOOA-PID score is 22.3% better than MOOA-PI. © 2024 Department of Agribusiness, Universitas Muhammadiyah Yogyakarta. All rights reserved.
Keywords: DC Microgrid; Droop Control; Metaheuristic; Optimization; Secondary Control

Authors: Dimas Ahmad Nur Kholis Suhermanto; Widi Aribowo; Hisham A. Shehadeh; Reza Rahmadian; Mahendra Widyartono; Ayusta Lukita Wardani; Aditya Chandra Hermawan
Title: Monitoring DC Motor Based on LoRa and IOT
Journal: Journal of Robotics and Control (JRC)
Abstract: Electrical energy efficiency is a dynamic in itself that continues to be driven by electrical energy providers. In this work, long-range (LoRa) technology is used to monitor DC motors. In the modern world, IoT is becoming increasingly prevalent. Embedded systems are now widely used in daily life. More can be done remotely in terms of control and monitoring. LoRa is a new technology discovered and developing rapidly. LoRa technology addresses the need for battery-operated embedded devices. LoRa technology is a long-range, low-power technology. In this investigation, a LoRa transmitter and a LoRa receiver were employed. This study employed a range of cases to test the LoRa device. In the first instance, there are no barriers, whereas there are in the second instance. The results of the two trials showed that the LoRa transmitter and receiver had successful communication. In this study, the room temperature is used to control DC motors. So that the DC motor's speed adjusts to fluctuations in the room's temperature. Additionally, measuring tools and the sensors utilised in this investigation were contrasted. The encoder sensor and the INA 219 sensor were the two measured sensors employed in this study. According to the findings of the experiment, the tool was functioning properly. © 2024 Department of Agribusiness, Universitas Muhammadiyah Yogyakarta. All rights reserved.
Keywords: DC Motor; INA219; IOT; Keywords—LoRa; Long-Range
TLDR: Room temperature is used to control DC motors so that the DC motor's speed adjusts to fluctuations in the room's temperature so that the DC motor's speed adjusts to fluctuations in the room's temperature.

Authors: Widi Aribowo; Hisham A. Shehadeh
Title: Novel modified Chernobyl disaster optimizer for controlling DC motor
Journal: Indonesian Journal of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science
Abstract: This article presents the modified Chernobyl disaster optimizer (CDO) method for DC motor control to find the optimal proportional integral derivative (PID) settings. DC motors are widely used machinery. DC motors are also simple to use. The detonation of the Chernobyl nuclear reactor core served as the inspiration for the idea and guiding principles of the CDO. CDO has limitations in the stability of exploration and exploitation areas. This research aims to obtain a new balance of exploration and exploitation. This study suggests incorporating the levy flight and chaotic algorithm (CA) techniques to enhance the CDO method. This study was conducted with the MATLAB/Simulink software. A comparative technique, which included the marine predator algorithm (MPA), golden jackal optimization (GJO), and CDO, was utilized to determine the performance of the MCDO method. According to the study's findings, the MCDO method's overshoot value outperformed all other approaches. © 2024 Institute of Advanced Engineering and Science. All rights reserved.
Keywords: Chernobyl disaster optimizer; DC motor; Metaheuristic; Optimization; Proportional integral derivative

Authors: B. Nur Vidia Laksmi; Muhammad Syahril Mubarok; Widi Aribowo; Mahendra Widyartono; Ayusta Lukita Wardani; Aditya Candra Hermawan
Title: Design of Forward DC-DC Converter for SCC using Cascaded Controller
Journal: 2024 7th International Conference on Vocational Education and Electrical Engineering: Charting the Course of Artificial Technology in Sustainable Society
Abstract: In this paper presented design of forward DC-DC converter for solar charging controller (SCC) implementation in renewable energy systems. It aims to optimize energy conversion efficiency and to enhance the performances. The renewable energy has recently become a prominent topic due environmentally friendly energy, as well as its advanced technology. The implementation of forward DC-DC converter as solar charging controller (SCC) is essential for regulating battery parameters and managing power flow from photovoltaic (PV) to battery. In addition, a cascaded proportional-integral (PI) controller is utilized to manage current and voltage parameters, thereby preventing battery overcharging. Control parameters are determined using the Ziegler-Nichols method reaction curve based. This method is used to obtained the precise control parameters. A MATLAB software is used for simulation and controller design State of charge (SoC) variations are presented in two scenarios: with and without the controller. The SoC levels are set at 2 5 %, 5 0 % and 9 5 %, where a high SoC percentage indicates a nearly full battery capacity, and a low SoC percentage indicates a nearly empty battery capacity. With the cascaded controller, both current and voltage responses across different SoC levels demonstrate satisfactory performance, including rapid transient responses, minimal overshoot, small ripples, and robustness. © 2024 IEEE.
Keywords: forward DC-DC converter; renewable energy; solar charging controller; state of charge

Authors: Mahendra Widyartono; Widi Aribowo; Reza Rahmadian; Ayusta Lukita Wardani; Aditya Chandra Hermawan
Title: Designing a portable solar generator for emergencies
Journal: E3S Web of Conferences
Abstract: Indonesia is a country made up of thousands of islands. Indonesia is located between four tectonic plates, the Indian Ocean Plate, and the Pacific Ocean Plate, the Asian Continental Plate, and the Australian Continental Plate. Geographically, the territory of Indonesia is in the series of Ring of Fire. The Ring of Fire, also called the Circum-Pacific Belt, is a 40,000 km-long chain of volcanoes and seismically active sites spanning the Pacific Ocean. This condition is very potential as well as prone to disasters such as volcanic eruptions, earthquakes, tsunamis, floods, and landslides. The impact of this disaster can make it difficult to access clean water, electricity, and fuel oil networks. Access to electricity is not available because the power grid is cut. The purpose of this research is to be able to provide first aid in terms of electricity so that it can relieve victims of natural disasters or volunteers who work to evacuate victims. The output targeted in this research is a Portable Solar Generator that can be used in an emergency by disaster-affected victims or by volunteers. With a 35-watt load, it could operate for 5 hours 30 minutes and with 55 Watt load it can operate for 3 hours. In terms of recharging time, the charging process using a 50 Wp solar panel takes approximately 7 hours, this is because the charging process is carried out outside the building according to actual conditions. © The Authors, published by EDP Sciences.

Authors: Widi Aribowo; Heri Suryoatmojo; Feby Agung Pamuji
Title: Novel hybrid of marine predator algorithm - Aquila optimizer for droop control in DC microgrid
Journal: International Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering
Abstract: This study presents a hybrid method, namely the marine predator algorithm (MPA) and Aquila optimizer (AO). The proposed algorithm is named MAO. AO duplicated the existence of the Aquila bird in nature while hunting for prey while MPA was inspired by predators in marine animal life. Although AO is widely accepted, it has several disadvantages. This causes various weaknesses such as a weak exploitation phase and slow growth of the convergence curve. Thus, certain exploitation and exploration in conventional AO can be studied to achieve the best balance. The MPA demonstrates the capacity to deliver optimal design and statistically efficient outcomes. The proposed method used AO as the main algorithm. To measure the performance of the proposed method, this study depicted a comparison using the AO, MPA, and whale optimization algorithm (WOA) methods. This paper was evaluated the performance of MAO on twenty-one CEC2017 benchmark functions test and droop control performance on direct current (DC) microgrid. From the simulation, MAO shows superior convergence ability. The proposed method and its application to droop control was successfully implemented and implied a promising performance. © 2024 Institute of Advanced Engineering and Science. All rights reserved.
Keywords: Aquila optimizer; DC microgrid; Droop control; Load sharing; Marine predator algorithm
